title: Add support for `--output` to `podman build --remote`
body:
### Feature request description

The local Podman client offers the `podman build --output` option to immediately save a built image to disk as a directory or a tar file. However, this was not implemented for the remote Podman client, which is a significant limitation (all Podman Desktop users are using remote Podman, for example).

### Suggest potential solution

I don't see a technical reason why we can't use the existing API for saving images to provide `build --output` support to the remote client.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

Use `podman --remote build` and `podman --remote save`

### Additional context

Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

comments.body:
@mheon 

Hi, I’ve been looking into this and wanted to sanity-check the implementation approach before I start working on it.

My current idea is to follow a flow similar to remote `podman save`:

1. Run the build on the server as usual.
2. Write the `--output` result to a temporary directroy/file on the server.
3. Stream/fetch that temporary artifact back to the remote client.
4. Materialize it at the client-side destination (`type=tar`, `type=local`, or stdout).
5. Clean up the temporary artifact on the server.

Does that sound like the right direction for this issue?
I was going to recommend using the existing `Export()` API bindings - which will stream directly from storage, instead of requiring you create the artifact on the server side and stream that?

> I was going to recommend using the existing `Export()` API bindings - which will stream directly from storage, instead of requiring you create the artifact on the server side and stream that?

Thanks for the reply.

My concern is more about whether `Export()` is the right abstraction here, since it seems unnecessary to go through saving the image first when the goal is to stream the final rootfs contents directly

Would it make more sense to introduce a separate function, something like `ExportRootFS()`, for streaming the final rootfs contents directly?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
